name: Dell XPS 15 (9530) Laptop
description: Dell's flagship 15-inch creator laptop pairs a Core i9-13900H processor with an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 GPU and a breathtaking 15.6-inch 3.5K OLED touch display. Premium aluminium-and-carbon-fibre construction, a near-borderless InfinityEdge display, and studio-quality audio make this the definitive Windows laptop for creative professionals.

specifications.value: Intel Core i9-13900H (14-core, up to 5.4 GHz)
15.6-inch 3.5K OLED touch, 3456 × 2160 px, 60 Hz, 400 nits, 100% DCI-P3
2× Thunderbolt 4, 1× USB-A 3.2, SD card reader, 3.5 mm audio, HDMI 2.1
Quad-speaker Waves MaxxAudio Pro, 2× 2.5 W woofers + 2× 1.5 W tweeters
Platinum Silver / Black carbon fibre palm rest

additionalInfo.value: Dell Technologies, One Dell Way, Round Rock, TX 78682

reviews.title: Best Windows laptop I've ever used
reviews.reviewText: The OLED screen is jaw-dropping — colours are vivid and blacks are true black. RTX 4070 handles 4K video editing smoothly and gaming at high settings is excellent. Build quality is exceptional for a laptop this thin. Highly recommended for creatives.

reviews.title: Stunning machine with a warm chassis
reviews.reviewText: Under sustained load the bottom gets noticeably warm. Not a dealbreaker, but worth knowing. The performance, screen, and build otherwise are exceptional — it's a beautiful laptop that produces beautiful work.

reviews.title: OLED display changes everything
reviews.reviewText: I edit photography professionally and the 100% DCI-P3 OLED display is the best colour-accurate screen I've used on a laptop. My edits look exactly the same when published. The i9 + RTX 4070 combo exports Lightroom catalogues faster than my desktop from two years ago.

reviews.title: Premium in every sense
reviews.reviewText: The carbon fibre palm rest, machined aluminium lid, and glass trackpad all feel luxurious. This is the best-built Windows laptop money can buy. The performance is everything I needed for my design work.

reviews.title: Great laptop, battery could be better
reviews.reviewText: The OLED screen is power-hungry and I get around 7–8 hours under mixed workloads rather than the stated 13. Still excellent by Windows standards. Performance is flawless and the build quality is impeccable.
